questions.question: Which of the following tools is NOT mentioned in the article as one you can connect to Notion?

options.explanation: Typeform is listed as an example integration for collecting participant information.
Salesforce is specifically mentioned as a tool that can reflect status changes in Notion.
Slack messages can be sent to a Notion database, as described in the article.
Microsoft Word is not referenced in the article; the listed tools are Typeform, Salesforce, Slack, GitHub, JIRA, Trello, Asana, etc.

questions.question: Where can you browse over 10,000 Notion templates according to the guide?

options.explanation: The article directs users to browse templates in the Template Gallery.
The Help Center provides articles, not a template library.
YouTube hosts videos, not a searchable template collection.
Zapier is for automation, not for browsing Notion page templates.

questions.question: How does the article suggest using Zapier with Notion?

options.text: To create new Notion pages from Gmail messages
options.explanation: The article does not mention Gmail integration via Zapier.

options.text: To reflect a status change in Salesforce in a Notion database
options.explanation: Zapier is cited as a way to sync Salesforce status changes to Notion.

options.text: To automatically sync Trello boards into Notion
options.explanation: While Trello links can be previewed, Zapier isn’t highlighted for that purpose.

options.text: To embed YouTube videos directly into Notion pages
options.explanation: Embedding videos is unrelated to Zapier’s automation role described here.

questions.question: Which feature does the guide highlight for keeping people informed about work happening outside of Notion?

options.explanation: APIs enable custom integrations but aren’t described as an information‑sharing feature.
The article specifically mentions using link previews to surface external work.
Email notifications are not discussed in the excerpt.
Push notifications are not referenced as a method for external work visibility.

questions.question: If you want to track progress on an Asana task inside Notion, what does the article recommend?

options.text: Use Zapier to sync Asana tasks automatically
options.explanation: Zapier is mentioned for Salesforce and Slack, not for Asana syncing.

options.text: Paste the Asana link into a Notion page to generate a preview
options.explanation: The guide says you can paste Asana links and get a preview that tracks progress.

options.text: Export Asana data as CSV and import it into Notion
options.explanation: CSV import is a possible method but not the one highlighted for quick visibility.

options.text: Write a custom integration using Notion’s API
options.explanation: Custom API work is possible but beyond the simple preview approach described.

questions.question: What is a primary advantage of building your own integrations with Notion’s API rather than relying solely on partner integrations?

options.text: It lets you automate any workflow without the limitations of pre‑made partner tools
options.explanation: Custom API integrations give full flexibility to design any automation, unlike fixed partner solutions.

options.text: It guarantees faster performance than all partner integrations
options.explanation: Performance depends on implementation; the article does not claim speed advantages.

options.text: It removes the need for authentication when accessing Notion data
options.explanation: All API calls still require proper authentication.

options.text: It provides built‑in analytics for your workflows
options.explanation: Analytics are not a built‑in feature of the API; they would need to be added separately.

questions.question: According to the article, which combination of tools can automatically capture messages posted in a Slack channel into a Notion database?

options.explanation: The article does not mention a native direct integration for this purpose.
Zapier is cited as a way to send Slack messages to a Notion database.
Typeform is used for form data collection, not for Slack message capture.

options.text: Embedding the Slack channel inside a Notion page
options.explanation: Embedding shows the channel but does not automatically store messages in a database.
